---
title: "Quiz 2"
format: typst
author: Name \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
---



# Color in Data Visualization


@. Which type of color palette would be most appropriate for visualizing **annual income data across a country**, where you want to show variation from low to high?

A) Qualitative  
B) Diverging  
C) Sequential  
D) Rainbow  



@. You are visualizing **political leaning data** scored from **−10 (far left) to +10 (far right)**, with **0 being neutral**. Which palette type is most appropriate **and why**?
 
A) Sequential — because the data is ordered  
B) Diverging — because there is a meaningful midpoint at 0  
C) Qualitative — because political labels are categories  
D) Rainbow — because it shows the full spectrum  



@. What does the **Adelson Checker Shadow illusion** demonstrate about color in data visualization?

A) Computers display color differently than humans see it  
B) Color perception is absolute and consistent across viewers  
C) The same color can appear different depending on surrounding context  
D) Bright colors are always more visible than dark colors  



@. Which of the following is a key benefit of **perceptually uniform color scales**?

A) They always use the most visually appealing colors  
B) They ensure equal data changes correspond to equal visual color differences  
C) They automatically adjust for colorblind viewers  
D) They reduce the file size of the visualization  



@. A researcher maps a **factor variable** to color in `ggplot2` but wants to manually assign specific colors to each level. Which function should they use?

A) `scale_color_continuous()`  
B) `scale_color_viridis_c()`  
C) `scale_color_manual()`  
D) `scale_color_gradient()`  




@. You want to show differences between **five unordered categories** — for example, five different bird species. Which palette type from **ColorBrewer** is most appropriate?

A) Sequential  
B) Diverging  
C) Qualitative  
D) Continuous  




Consider the following code:

```{.r}
library(tidyverse)
ggplot(demo_df, aes(x, y, color = double_var)) +
geom_point(size = 4) +
scale_color_continuous(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue")
```

@. What does changing `scale_color_continuous()` affect?

A) The underlying data values  
B) The position of points on the plot  
C) Only the visual color mapping, not the data or structure  
D) The shape of the geom  


@. Which of the following **R packages** provides color palettes that are specifically designed to be **perceptually uniform AND colorblind-friendly**?

A) `wesanderson`  
B) `viridis`  
C) `ggsci`  
D) `RColorBrewer`  
 

# Preattentive Attributes & Gestalt Principles



@. A scatterplot contains **200 gray points** and **one red point** representing an outlier. The red point immediately draws attention without effort.

Which concept explains this effect?

A) Chunking  
B) Preattentive processing  
C) Closure  
D) Continuity  



@. In a grouped bar chart, bars representing the **same year** are placed close together, while bars from different years have extra spacing between them.

Which Gestalt principle is primarily being used?

A) Similarity  
B) Proximity  
C) Closure  
D) Figure & Ground  




A report consistently colors **Republicans red** and **Democrats blue** across all figures.

@. Which Gestalt principle helps viewers interpret this correctly?

A) Continuity  
B) Similarity  
C) Enclosure  
D) Connectivity  



@. Why are **line charts** especially effective for showing trends over time?

A) They reduce working memory usage by chunking  
B) They rely on the principle of continuity  
C) They use enclosure to group observations  
D) They emphasize symmetry between values  



@. A visualization surrounds related variables with a **light gray background rectangle** to show they belong to the same category.

Which Gestalt principle is being applied?

A) Proximity  
B) Similarity  
C) Enclosure  
D) Closure  



@. Why is highlighting **only one important element** with color often recommended?

A) It reduces file size  
B) It prevents similarity effects  
C) It keeps viewers in fast preattentive processing mode  
D) It eliminates the need for labels  



@. A scatterplot uses **larger point size** to emphasize the most important observations.

Which category of preattentive attribute is being used?

A) Color  
B) Spatial  
C) Form  
D) Motion  


# Spatial Data & Choropleth Maps


@. Which of the following best describes **geospatial data**?

A) Data that only includes latitude and longitude  
B) Data describing relationships between variables over time only  
C) Data that includes location, attributes, and sometimes time information  
D) Data stored exclusively as satellite images  


@. Vector data represents __________ geographic features.

A) continuous  
B) discrete  
C) simulated  
D) interpolated  



@. Which type of vector geometry would be most appropriate for representing **roads or rivers**?

A) Points  
B) Lines  
C) Polygons  
D) Raster cells  



@. Which of the following is an example of **raster data**?

A) State boundaries  
B) River networks  
C) Satellite temperature grid  
D) City coordinates  


Consider the following R code:

```{.r}
ggplot(us_states, aes(long, lat, group = group, fill = region)) +
geom_polygon(color = "white")
```

@. What role does `group = group` play in this map?

A) Assigns projection coordinates  
B) Connects polygon boundary points correctly  
C) Controls legend placement  
D) Normalizes spatial data  



@. A map projection is a mathematical transformation from a __________ to a flat surface.

A) dataset  
B) raster grid  
C) globe  
D) coordinate table  



@. Which projection type is **best suited for statistical comparison across regions**?

A) Mercator  
B) Equal-area projection  
C) Robinson projection  
D) Perspective projection  



@. Which of the following is a defining characteristic of a **choropleth map**?

A) Uses symbols sized by magnitude  
B) Displays raster imagery only  
C) Shades geographic regions according to a quantitative variable  
D) Connects spatial points using lines
